{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.4": 3, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 2, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The critique identifies three prior works (FreeForm-CoT, Chain-of-Thought, Zero-shot CoT) but doesn't delve into specific deltas compared to StepWise-CoT.",
    "RX.1": "The critique focuses on the weaknesses of the presented work without explicitly stating uncertainties about its claims.",
    "RX.3": "The critique addresses the scope by mentioning the comparison with baselines, but it doesn't directly assess whether the output covers exactly the agreed-upon set of dimensions.",
    "RX.4": "The critique highlights potential harm in overstating the results and generalizability of StepWise-CoT, suggesting a minor regression in terms of responsible AI practices.",
    "RX.5": "The critique doesn't discuss provenance or traceability of the presented arguments."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.4": 0.7, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.6, "RX.4": 0.8, "RX.5": 0.4},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The critique identifies three prior works (FreeForm-CoT, Chain-of-Thought, Zero-shot CoT) but doesn't delve into specific deltas compared to StepWise-CoT.
rationale.RX.1: The critique focuses on the weaknesses of the presented work without explicitly stating uncertainties about its claims.
rationale.RX.3: The critique addresses the scope by mentioning the comparison with baselines, but it doesn't directly assess whether the output covers exactly the agreed-upon set of dimensions.
rationale.RX.4: The critique highlights potential harm in overstating the results and generalizability of StepWise-CoT, suggesting a minor regression in terms of responsible AI practices.
rationale.RX.5: The critique doesn't discuss provenance or traceability of the presented arguments.
